perturbation.original_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon Participant with respect to Confidential Information that (a) was known to Participant before receipt from Google, as evidenced by Participant's files and records in existence before the time of disclosure; (b) is or becomes publicly available through no fault of Participant; (c) is rightfully received by Participant from a third party without a duty of confidentiality; (d) is disclosed by Google to a third party without a duty of confidentiality on the third party; or (e) is disclosed by Participant with Google's prior written approval.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement imposes no obligation upon Participant with respect to Confidential Information that (a) was known to Participant before receipt from Google, as evidenced by Participant's oral testimony; (b) is or becomes publicly available regardless of Participant fault; (c) is received by Participant from a third party; (d) is disclosed by Google to a third party; or (e) is disclosed by Participant with presumed approval.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguities that contradict the original intent by weakening the exceptions to confidentiality. Replacing 'Participant's files and records' with 'oral testimony' for prior knowledge makes it harder to verify. Removing 'without a duty of confidentiality' from third-party disclosures, and presumed approval, and 'through no fault of Participant' creates uncertainty and contradictions the obligation around the confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between Google Inc., for itself and its subsidiaries and affiliates ("Google"), and ____________, for themselves and any company they may represent ("Participant"). In connection with Participant's visit, Google shall disclose or expose to Participant all information, including but not limited to proprietary information that is related to the business purpose of the visit. This information shall be considered Google’s "Confidential Information". Participant may use the Confidential Information only for the purpose associated with Participant's visit, or any other reason that they may find relevant for any business reason. Participant will use the same degree of care as Participant uses with respect to its own similar information to protect the Confidential Information; provided, however, Participant shall not be liable for reasonable accidental disclosure. If it's disclosed that information was leaked by the participant due to negligence, they will not be held reliable for that action, if done with no bad intentions. This Agreement imposes no obligation upon Participant with respect to Confidential Information that (a) was known to Participant before receipt from Google, as evidenced by Participant's oral testimony; (b) is or becomes publicly available regardless of Participant fault; (c) is received by Participant from a third party; (d) is disclosed by Google to a third party; or (e) is disclosed by Participant with presumed approval. If Confidential Information is required to be produced by law, court order, or governmental authority, Participant must immediately notify Google of that obligation. Participant acknowledges and understands that hardware, technology, source code, and software to which Participant may have access or which are disclosed to Participant as a result of his/her visit to Google may be subject to U.S. export control laws and regulations. Participant hereby certifies that he/she shall not -- directly or indirectly -- sell, export, reexport, transfer, divert, or otherwise dispose of any hardware, software, source code, or technology (including products derived from or based on such technology or software) received from Google to any country (or national thereof) without obtaining prior authorization from Google and the appropriate government authorities. In addition, Participant certifies that he/she is not a citizen of Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Sudan, or Syria. This Agreement shall remain in effect until such time as all Confidential Information of Google disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of Participant. Participant, upon Google's written request, will promptly return all Confidential Information received from Google, together with all copies, or certify in writing that all such Confidential Information and copies thereof have been destroyed. This Agreement between the parties supersedes any other prior or collateral agreements, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof and constitutes the entire agreement with respect to the subject matter hereof. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California, without reference to conflict of laws principles. The exclusive venue for any dispute relating to this Agreement shall be in the state or federal courts within Santa Clara County, California. Signature: __________________________________ Print Name: _________________________________ Address: ___________________________________ _______________________________________ Date: ______________________________________
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the exceptions to the confidentiality agreement, which contradicts the original intent of the agreement to protect Google's confidential information. Specifically, replacing 'Participant's files and records' with 'oral testimony' makes it harder to verify prior knowledge. Removing 'without a duty of confidentiality' from third-party disclosures and presumed approval creates uncertainty. Removing 'through no fault of Participant' creates uncertainty and contradictions the obligation around the confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: Participant hereby certifies that he/she shall not -- directly or indirectly -- sell, export, reexport, transfer, divert, or otherwise dispose of any hardware, software, source code, or technology (including products derived from or based on such technology or software) received from Google to any country (or national thereof) without obtaining prior authorization from Google and the appropriate government authorities. In addition, Participant certifies that he/she is not a citizen of Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Sudan, or Syria.
perturbation.changed_text: Participant hereby agrees to only make reasonable efforts to avoid selling, exporting, re-exporting, transferring, diverting, or otherwise disposing of any hardware, software, source code, or technology (including products derived from or based on such technology or software) received from Google to any country (or national thereof) without consulting Google. In addition, Participant certifies that he/she may be a citizen of Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Sudan, or Syria, but is not acting on behalf of those countries.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall not' to 'agrees to only make reasonable efforts to avoid' introduces ambiguity and weakens the original prohibition of exporting or transferring technology. The statement about citizenship directly contradicts the original certification, creating a contradiction in the agreement regarding compliance with export regulations. This contradicts the original intent to prevent the transfer of technology to restricted countries. Participant acknowledges and understands that hardware, technology, source code, and software to which Participant may have access or which are disclosed to Participant as a result of his/her visit to Google may be subject to U.S. export control laws and regulations.

In connection with Participant's visit, Google shall disclose or expose to Participant all information, including but not limited to proprietary information that is related to the business purpose of the visit. This information shall be considered Google’s "Confidential Information". Signature: __________________________________ Print Name: _________________________________ Address: ___________________________________ _______________________________________ Date: ______________________________________
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the original prohibition against exporting technology to restricted countries, replacing 'shall not' with 'agrees to only make reasonable efforts to avoid' and 'without obtaining prior authorization' with 'without consulting Google.' Furthermore, it alters the citizenship certification from a strict denial to a conditional allowance ('may be a citizen...but is not acting on behalf of those countries'). The contradicted text expects adherence to U.S. export control laws and regulations, but the changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the commitment to compliance. This creates a strong contradiction, as the original text implies strict adherence, while the changed text allows for potential non-compliance with only 'reasonable efforts,' rendering enforcement uncertain. The original text, which includes "Participant acknowledges and understands that hardware, technology, source code, and software to which Participant may have access or which are disclosed to Participant as a result of his/her visit to Google may be subject to U.S. export control laws and regulations." implies an understanding of these laws, whereas the change now makes it possible for the participant to not follow them based on 'reasonable efforts.'

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect until such time as all Confidential Information of Google disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of Participant.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of one year, regardless of whether Confidential Information of Google disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through any action or inaction of Participant.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction by setting a fixed duration (one year) for the agreement, overriding the original condition that the agreement remains in effect until the Confidential Information becomes publicly known. This creates confusion about the duration of the confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the initial intention of protecting the Google's Confidential Information even if all the time of disclosing has pass.

Signature: __________________________________ Print Name: _________________________________ Address: ___________________________________ _______________________________________ Date: ______________________________________
perturbation.justification: The changed text sets a fixed one-year duration for the agreement, regardless of whether the confidential information becomes public knowledge. This directly contradicts the original intent, which was to protect Google's confidential information indefinitely, even if the disclosure period has passed. The contradicted text, which is also the changed text in this instance, now contains conflicting statements about the agreement's duration, creating a clear in-text contradiction, thereby creating ambiguity, which falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The exclusive venue for any dispute relating to this Agreement shall be in the state or federal courts within Santa Clara County, California.
perturbation.changed_text: The venue for any dispute relating to this Agreement shall be in any court of competent jurisdiction, without reference to location.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'exclusive venue in Santa Clara County, California' to 'any court of competent jurisdiction, without reference to location' introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the original venue provision. This uncertainty about the jurisdiction where disputes will be resolved can lead to legal complications. The original agreement made sure that the only place to resolve any disputes would be California.

perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the exclusive venue for disputes is Santa Clara County, California. The changed text broadens this to 'any court of competent jurisdiction, without reference to location.' This directly contradicts the original venue provision in the NDA, as it removes the exclusivity of Santa Clara County and introduces the possibility of disputes being resolved in any location. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because both sections are regarding the same venue for disputes, but contradict each other.
